text:
Morshu:

1st form: No song, but very powerful with:
Lamp oil: Slips the opponent AND does 500 damage
Rope: Traps the player, deals no damage
Bombs: 700 damage

2nd form: 1/2 song, 10-20% more powerful. Gains a new move
MMMMMMMMMMM: Constantly annoys the player, making them gain 50% ATTACK, but, makes them so annoyed they have 75% chance to hit themselves.

Third form: FULL song, significantly stronger by 65-70% from original form. Gains the power of stepping on the beach and Daft Punk. Gains 3 moves.
Rubies: Gestures the player to give money, in return for a free ATTACK. It's a trap, as he attacks during the sequence. 300 damage
Stepping on the beach: Uses power of SpongeBob to step on the player as they are covered on sand. 450 damage
Daft Robot Punk: Sends Daft Punk to attack the player. 370 damage.

Final form: MORSHU IS SERIOUS! Song is backwards, his eyes are glowing in rainbow color. He is hovering over the air like a god. 6 new attacks.
BOMB frenzy: Traps the player and slips them with oil and rope, and drops down 4-5 bombs on the opponent. 700 damage.
Daft sponge: Combines Daft Punk and SpongeBob to turn them into a Robot-Sponge Hybrid. Hybrid attacks. 500 damage each bite. Have to kill to banish from play
Oil frenzy: Slips the player constantly, with 100 damage each slip.
Rope frenzy: 3 modes. 1 mode up each successful attack miss. Original rope: Traps the player. Barbed rope: Scrapes and traps the player. 200 damage. Barbed shock rope: Shocks the opponent for 200 damage, barbs hit for 300. 
Eye rubies: Shoots rubies from eyes. Opponent takes 300-325 damage per ruby.
The final choice... Upon being on his last breath, Morshu instantly blocks the final blow, reflecting it back to the opponent dealing the exact damage. Keep attacking until his [ENERGY 200000/200000] is zero. You then beat him.

This was an idea made by me in about 8 minutes. This should be a boss for a game ._.

text: ​ @Plutoniumcube  I think my original comment was a reference to something, but I cannot for the life of me remember what that something was. One thing is for certain, though: In spite of the passage of time, YTPs will continue to live on, some as glimpses into the eras of their creation, and others as just random BS that the creator found funny at the time. The past 10 years truly have been a rollercoaster of chaos, and it appears that there's no end to this track in sight. Keep your chin up, though, because we all make it sooner or later.
